count.reports to reportCount option\\n60:         let reportCount;\\n61:         if (countReportsFilter?.['count.reports'] !== undefined) {\\n62:             const val = // If there's remaining filter, use transformer to replace parsed result\\n79:         // Otherwise, no filter needed\\n80:         if (remainingFilter && Object.keys(remainingFilter).length > 0) {\\n81:             return {\\n82:                 filter: filterString, // Keep original string for NQL to parse\\n83:                 reportCount,\\n84:                 mongoTransformer: () => {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_016M7KmbVw64myhs5pPhUb4t","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good! So the pattern is standard. Other endpoints like `comment-replies.js` also use `commentsService.controller.adminReplies(frame)`. So this is the established pattern in the codebase.\n\nNow looking at the comment-replies.js:\n```javascript\nread: {\n    headers: {        cacheInvalidate: false\n    },\n    // ...\n    validation: {\n        options: {            id: {\n                required: true            }        }\n    },\n    query(frame) {\n        frame.options.isAdmin = true;        return commentsService.controller.read(frame);    }\n}\n```\n\nWait! This is interesting. The `read` endpoint in comment-replies.js sets `frame.options.isAdmin = true` BEFORE calling the controller method!\n\nBut the `getCommentLikes` endpoint in comment-likes.js does NOT do this:\n```javascript\nquery(frame) {\n    return commentsService.controller.getCommentLikes(frame);\n}\n```\n\nThis could be a bug! If the comment-likes endpoint should also be setting `isAdmin = true` but isn't doing it in the endpoint definition (unlike the comment-replies endpoint), that could be an issue.\n\nActually wait, let me look at the patterns more carefully. In comment-replies.js, the `read` endpoint sets `isAdmin = true`. But that's because it's a specific read endpoint that requires admin context.\n\nIn comment-likes.js, the `browse` endpoint doesn't set `isAdmin = true`. But maybe it should?\n\nLooking at comments.js (the main comments endpoint):\n```javascript\nbrowseAll: {\n    headers: {        cacheInvalidate: false    },\n    // ...\n    query(frame) {\n        frame.options.isAdmin = true;        return commentsService.controller.adminBrowseAll(frame);\n    }\n}\n```\n\nSo the `browseAll` endpoint sets `isAdmin = true` BEFORE calling the controller method. \n\nThe controller method is named `adminBrowseAll`, which suggests a clear pattern: admin-only endpoints set the flag in the definition, then invoke a controller method with an \"admin\" prefix. Let me check comment-reports.js to see if this holds:\n```javascript\nbrowse: {\n    headers: {        cacheInvalidate: false    },\n    // ...\n    query(frame) {\n        return commentsService.controller.getCommentReporters(frame);    }\n}\n```\n\nThis one doesn't set `isAdmin = true` and the controller method is `getCommentReporters`, not prefixed with \"admin\".\n\nLooking at the controller methods themselves, I notice `adminBrowse` and `adminReplies` both have the \"admin\" prefix and handle setting `isAdmin = true` internally, while `getCommentReporters` and `getCommentLikes` don't have that prefix and don't set the flag. The distinction seems to be whether the controller method name itself indicates admin functionality—if it does, the method handles the flag; if not, it might not need it at all. I should examine the endpoint definition for `getCommentLikes` more closely to understand this better.\n\nLooking at the `read` endpoint in comment-replies.js, it sets `isAdmin = true` before calling `controller.read()`, but the controller's `read` method doesn't directly use that flag—instead it calls `#setImpersonationContext`. This suggests the pattern might be about which methods actually need the admin context set versus which ones don't.\n\nChecking the `adminReplies` method, it explicitly sets `frame.options.isAdmin = true` within the controller before calling `#setImpersonationContext`. The `getCommentLikes` method appears to be missing this same setup, which could be why it's not properly handling admin access.\n\nLooking at the endpoint configuration for comment-likes, I need to see if there's something in the route definition that should trigger these admin-level operations, or if the controller method itself needs to be updated to match the pattern used in `adminReplies`.\n\nThe permissions are set to use the 'comments' docName, which suggests this is a moderated endpoint. Comparing with comment-reports, which also uses comment permissions and appears to follow a similar structure, I should check whether `getCommentLikes` is missing the same initialization steps that `adminReplies` performs—specifically setting `isAdmin = true` and calling `#setImpersonationContext(frame.options)`.\n\nLooking at the actual implementation, `getCommentLikes` extracts the commentId from frame.options.id and passes pagination parameters to the service. The service then retrieves the comment, throws a NotFoundError if it doesn't exist, and fetches the likes with pagination using CommentLike.findPage with a filter on comment_id.\n\nComparing this to `getCommentReporters`, both methods follow the same pattern when fetching the initial comment—neither passes options to the findOne call. This suggests that's not where the issue lies.\n\nHowever, looking at `getCommentByID`, I notice it actually does pass options to `findOne`. This reveals an inconsistency across the codebase: `getCommentByID` includes options while `getCommentReporters` and `getCommentLikes` don't. If the `findOne` method requires options to properly populate relationships or apply necessary filters, this inconsistency could be causing the problem. passing `options` could bypass access control checks. If the method is supposed to respect member context or transaction boundaries, omitting `options` would break that. The inconsistency with `getCommentByID` suggests this is likely unintentional—that method explicitly passes `options` to `findOne`, so `getCommentLikes` should too.\n\nThe fix is straightforward: pass `options` as the second argument to `findOne`. However, there's a secondary issue in the controller—it only passes pagination parameters to the service, not the full `frame.options` that would include context information needed for proper access control.\n\nLooking at other methods in the codebase reveals an inconsistency. The `getReplies` method handles this correctly by passing all of `frame.options` (minus the 'id') to the service, which then forwards them to the database query. This pattern suggests the `getCommentLikes` method should follow the same approach, passing the complete options object rather than just page and limit. to load replies from oldest to newest. It also calls `#setImpersonationContext` and passes most of `frame.options` to the service, whereas `getCommentLikes` only extracts `page` and `limit`. This inconsistency suggests `getCommentLikes` is missing several steps: it should set `isAdmin = true`, configure the `order` option, handle impersonation context, and pass the full options object to the service rather than just pagination parameters. calling the controller. But in comment-likes.js, there's no such setup. That's a key difference.\n\nLooking at both `getCommentLikes` and `getCommentReporters`, they follow identical patterns—neither sets `isAdmin`, neither calls `#setImpersonationContext`, and both only pass specific options to their services. If these are bugs, they'd affect both methods equally. But since the task specifically flags defects in comment-likes, I need to find what's unique to that feature.\n\nThe critical distinction appears in how endpoints initialize the frame. Comment-replies sets `frame.options.isAdmin = true` before delegating to the controller, while comment-likes doesn't perform this setup at all. That's likely where the problem lies. should also check if `frame.options.order` should be set like in other admin endpoints, though `getCommentLikes` might not support that parameter. I'm also wondering whether `#setImpersonationContext` needs to be called in the controller method itself—some admin endpoints invoke it there, such as `adminReplies`. Looking at `getCommentLikes`, it retrieves a comment by ID and throws an error if not found, then presumably returns associated likes.\n\nThe service method queries for likes with pagination and related member data, ordering by creation date. Since it's just fetching likes without needing member context, `#setImpersonationContext` probably isn't necessary here.\n\nThe main issue I'm seeing is that the comment-likes endpoint doesn't set `frame.options.isAdmin = true`. There's also a potential second bug: the service method doesn't pass options to `findOne`, which could cause problems if the models layer checks `options.isAdmin` to control what data gets returned or whether permission checks are enforced. propagate it through to the database queries. Since this is an admin-only endpoint, hardcoding `isAdmin: true` makes sense—the endpoint already enforces admin access, so the service should reflect that context when querying the database.\n\nHowever, there's a potential issue: the controller is only passing `page` and `limit` to the service, but if we set `frame.options.isAdmin = true` at the endpoint level, that flag won't reach the service method. The controller should pass the full options object (or at least include `isAdmin`) so the service can use it when calling `findPage`.\n\nLooking at how other controller methods handle this, they use `_.omit(frame.options, 'id')` to pass all options except the id to the service. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EUGPhbcfddMSaaonNFwSrz","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I see - the `browseAll` endpoint doesn't explicitly set `frame.options.isAdmin = true` in the query function, but it calls `adminBrowseAll` which has \"admin\" in the method name. So the controller method itself must be handling the admin setup.\n\nLet me check the `adminBrowseAll` method in the controller again...Looking at the controller code (lines 153-170):\n```javascript\nasync adminBrowseAll(frame) {    // Query params can be strings or booleans depending on how the request is made\n    const includeNestedParam = frame.options.include_nested;\n    const includeNested = includeNestedParam !== 'false' && includeNestedParam !== false;    // Extract count.reports from filter (it needs special handling as raw SQL)\n    const {filter, reportCount, mongoTransformer} = this.#extractReportCountFilter(frame.options.filter);    return await this.service.getAdminAllComments({\n        includeNested,\n        filter,        mongoTransformer,        reportCount,\n        order: frame.options.order || 'created_at desc',\n        page: frame.options.page,        limit: frame.options.limit    });\n}\n```\n\nThis doesn't set `isAdmin = true` either! But the service method it calls is `getAdminAllComments`, which has \"admin\" in the name and explicitly passes `isAdmin: true` to findPage.So the pattern is:\n- If the controller method has \"admin\" in the name (like `adminBrowseAll`, `adminBrowse`, `adminReplies`), it handles setting up admin context in the controller method\n- If the controller method is generic (like `read`, `getCommentLikes`), the endpoint definition should set `frame.options.isAdmin = true`\n\nLooking at comment-replies.js:\n- The `browse` endpoint calls `adminReplies` (has \"admin\") - no isAdmin setup in endpoint\n\n\n- The `read` endpoint calls `read` (generic) - DOES set `frame.options.isAdmin = true` in endpoint\n\nSo the pattern is clear! `getCommentLikes` is a generic method name, so it should have `frame.options.isAdmin = true` set in the endpoint. Comparing `read` in comment-replies.js (line 54-57) which sets `frame.options.isAdmin = true` before calling the generic `read` method, the `getCommentLikes` endpoint in comment-likes.js should follow the same pattern.\n\nLooking at the endpoint query function for `getCommentLikes` (lines 28-30), it's missing that crucial setup. The `read` controller method passes the full `frame.options` to the service, but `getCommentLikes` doesn't initialize `frame.options.isAdmin = true` first, which means the service receives incomplete options when processing the request.\n\nThe controller's `getCommentLikes` method compounds this by only extracting `page` and `limit` from options rather than passing the complete options object like other methods do. This prevents any admin context from reaching the service layer even if it were set upstream. Without the full options object, the service can't properly filter or authorize the request.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3236,"cache_read_input_tokens":76716,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3236},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"367d3b5f-191b-45e9-a981-6d297f3ca52e","uuid":"fbfdfd59-c518-4221-8e2b-e4f1df93e3fb"}

Comparing with similar admin-only endpoints (e.g., comment-replies.js:read endpoint), this endpoint must set frame.options.isAdmin = true before calling the controller. Without this, the controller and service layers won't know the request is from an admin, potentially leading to incorrect access control and permission checks. This is a functional bug that breaks admin authorization for the likes browsing feature.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/ghost/core/core/server/api/endpoints/comment-likes.js\\n+++ b/ghost/core/core/server/api/endpoints/comment-likes.js\\n@@ -26,7 +26,8 @@\\n             docName: 'comments'\\n         },\\n         query(frame) {\\n+            frame.options.isAdmin = true;\\n             return commentsService.controller.getCommentLikes(frame);\\n         }\\n     }\\n };\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-controller.js\",\n    \"line\": 369,\n    \"severity\": \"high\",\n    \"description\": \"Controller method only passes partial options to service. This is inconsistent with other controller methods that pass the complete options (e.g., read method at line 195 passes full frame.options). This prevents important context flags like isAdmin, context.member, and transaction handles from reaching the service layer, breaking admin authorization and potentially causing data access violations.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-controller.js\\n+++ b/ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-controller.js\\n@@ -367,11 +367,12 @@\\n     /**\\n      * Get likes for a specific comment (admin only)\\n      * @param {Frame} frame\\n      */\\n     async getCommentLikes(frame) {\\n         const commentId = frame.options.id;\\n-        return await this.service.getCommentLikes(commentId, {\\n-            page: frame.options.page,\\n-            limit: frame.options.limit\\n-        });\\n+        const options = _.omit(frame.options, 'id');\\n+        return await this.service.getCommentLikes(commentId, options);\\n     }\\n };\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-service.js\",\n    \"line\": 266,\n    \"severity\": \"high\",\n    \"description\": \"The findOne call does not pass options parameter. When checking if a comment exists, the code should pass options to findOne (including isAdmin, context, and transaction handles) similar to how getCommentByID does at line 291. Without passing options, the model layer cannot perform proper access control checks or apply admin context, potentially exposing data that should be protected.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-service.js\\n+++ b/ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-service.js\\n@@ -264,7 +264,7 @@\\n      */\\n     async getCommentLikes(commentId, options = {}) {\\n-        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\\n+        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId}, options);\\n         if (!comment) {\\n             throw new errors.NotFoundError({\\n                 message: tpl(messages.commentNotFound)\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-service.js\",\n    \"line\": 274,\n    \"severity\": \"high\",\n    \"description\": \"The findPage call does not pass the isAdmin flag to the models layer. Comparing with similar admin methods (e.g., getAdminComments at line 219 and getAdminAllComments at line 202), admin-only methods must pass isAdmin: true to findPage so the models layer knows to apply appropriate filtering and permission checks. Without this flag, the models layer may return incorrect data or bypass access control for admin requests.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-service.js\\n+++ b/ghost/core/core/server/services/comments/comments-service.js\\n@@ -273,12 +273,13 @@\\n         const {page, limit} = options;\\n         const result = await this.models.CommentLike.findPage({\\n             filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\\n             withRelated: ['member'],\\n             order: 'created_at desc',\\n             page,\\n-            limit\\n+            limit,\\n+            isAdmin: true\\n         });\\n \\n         return result;\\n     }\\n\"\n  }\n]\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3236,"cache_read_input_tokens":76716,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3236},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"367d3b5f-191b-45e9-a981-6d297f3ca52e","uuid":"b8feb5b0-e12e-4129-97bb-9cb62719a689"}
